methodology: Parallel cartography (surveyor + compass subagents) plus direct smart_grep/read_source deep dives
key_discovery: The codebase migrated from SQLite to PGlite (embedded PostgreSQL). Zero better-sqlite3, zero drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3, zero sqliteTable. The #db alias resolves to db.pg.ts (PGlite + node-postgres). All .pg.sql.ts schema files use pgTable from drizzle-orm/pg-core.

findings.reason: Exports `dualWriteAdapterLayer` and `makeDualWriteAdapter` — neither is imported by any file in packages/opencode/. The module itself imports makeLocalPgAdapter, makePgAdapter, Service, and several types from ./adapter, all of which are live imports in adapter.ts. But NOTHING imports from adapter-dual-write.ts. The app runtime (src/effect/app-runtime.ts:91) uses `DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer` which is defined in adapter.ts (the PGlite-only path). 122 lines of dead code.
findings.risk: Carries unused dependencies, adds surface area for confusion, and contains the misleading 'SQLite is primary' comment (line 25) when SQLite is no longer the backend.

findings.reason: Type `_SQLiteSatisfiesDLC` at line 409 — a compile-time structural type assertion: `type _SQLiteSatisfiesDLC = DrizzleLikeClient extends any ? true : never`. This evaluates to `true` unconditionally (it checks whether DrizzleLikeClient is assignable to `any`). It is a no-op compile-time assertion with a misleading name referencing SQLite. Never referenced elsewhere (grep confirms it only appears at its definition). The comment on line 410 says 'SQLite client satisfies via duck typing' but the actual live code uses PGlite which has a different Drizzle interface. The TODO on line 412 confirms a future PgClient type assertion is needed instead.
findings.action: Remove this dead type assertion. Replace with the planned `_PgSatisfiesDLC` assertion once PgClient from db.pg.ts is stabilized.

findings.reason: `export type Transaction = any` — marked `@deprecated` with the note 'any type provides no type safety.' The comment explains it should be replaced with `PgliteTransaction | NodePgTransaction` from drizzle-orm/pglite / drizzle-orm/node-postgres. This is actively deprecated dead code. `TxOrDb` (line 92) depends on it: `type TxOrDb = Transaction | Client`. Used at runtime via the `any` escape hatch — not truly dead at runtime but dead in design intent.
findings.action: Replace `Transaction = any` with the proper union from drizzle-orm/pglite and drizzle-orm/node-postgres per the deprecation comment.

findings.reason: Two outdated comments referencing SQLite: (1) Line 27: 'Windows can keep SQLite WAL handles alive until GC finalizers run' — PGlite uses PostgreSQL, not SQLite WAL; (2) Line 79: '// Use in-memory sqlite' — actually sets OPENCODE_DB=:memory: which triggers in-memory PGlite, not SQLite. These comments are misleading and stale.
findings.action: Update comments to reference PGlite instead of SQLite.

findings.reason: `export { Database } from '@/storage/db'` — marked `@deprecated use DatabaseAdapter.Service instead — direct Database access bypasses the adapter layer`. Still exported in the public API but deprecated. 21 source files still import `Database` from `@/storage/db` (the convenience re-export of drizzle-orm). The deprecation is aspirational — the export is still heavily used.
findings.action: Complete the migration of all 21 consumers to DatabaseAdapter.Service before removing this export.

vestigial_naming.reason: `SQLiteTransactionOptions` type and `sqliteOptions` variable — this is LIVE code with vestigial naming. PGlite accepts SQLite-compatible transaction behavior options (deferred/immediate/exclusive), so the discriminated union tag `_tag: 'sqlite'` is actually correct at the protocol level. Used in `makeLocalPgAdapter()` at line 123 to extract behavior options for `Database.transaction()`. Also used in test at adapter.unit.test.ts:86.
vestigial_naming.action: Could rename to `PgTransactionOptions` with `_tag: 'pglite'` for clarity, but the SQLite-compatible naming is semantically accurate for PGlite's API.

vestigial_naming.reason: Variable named `sqliteAdapter` — actually wraps a PGlite adapter. The entire file is dead code (see DEAD-001), but within the dead code, the variable naming is misleading.
vestigial_naming.action: No action needed — entire file should be removed per DEAD-001.

vestigial_naming.reason: Comments say 'SQLite is primary' and 'SQLite-only' — PGlite is the actual backend. Dead code with misleading comments.
vestigial_naming.action: No action needed — entire file should be removed per DEAD-001.

type_only_artifacts.reason: `SQLiteTransactionOptions` type (lines 53-56) used in the `TransactionOptions` union (line 64-65). Exported but only used within adapter.ts itself to define `TransactionOptions`. The type is necessary for the discriminated union — removing it would break `TransactionOptions`. However, the `_tag: 'sqlite'` is semantically about PGlite's SQLite-compatible API.
type_only_artifacts.action: Keep; the type is needed. Consider renaming to PgTransactionOptions if clarity is desired.

active_references.reason: `import { Database } from 'bun:sqlite'` — ONLY file using bun:sqlite. Used to create Zed editor fixture databases for testing `resolveZedSelection()`. This reads Zed's own SQLite workspace database — completely separate from opencode's application database. Active test code.
`'sqlite'` in the binary file extension Set. Used to detect binary files during file listing. Not database-related — file classification.
`SQLiteTransactionOptions` and `sqliteOptions` variable — live code in `makeLocalPgAdapter()` for PGlite transaction behavior. See VEST-001.
`.db` file extension for database file paths. Uses `.db` extension but the underlying engine is PGlite (PostgreSQL). Active.
Test exercising SQLite-compatible transaction options: `{ _tag: 'sqlite', behavior: 'immediate' }`. Active test.
Test return value `'sqlite-ok'` — just a test string, not a SQLite reference. Active test.
Smoke test regex matching `.sqlite` extension as a valid DB path. Active test.
`.db` extension for exercise test DB path. Active test infrastructure.
The `#db` alias target — PGlite + node-postgres initialization. Uses drizzle-orm/pglite and drizzle-orm/node-postgres. ZERO SQLite. This is the active database engine.
Drizzle config with `dialect: 'postgresql'` — no SQLite dialect. Active.

deleted_files_detected: editor-context-zed.test.ts references 'stubs for deleted modules' (isZedTerminal, offsetToPosition, resolveZedDbPath, resolveZedSelection) — these were Zed integration source files that have been deleted; the test now uses stubs.
recommendations: Remove adapter-dual-write.ts (122 lines of dead code)
Replace Transaction = any with proper Postgres transaction union type
Update misleading 'SQLite' comments in test/preload.ts to reference PGlite
Complete the DatabaseAdapter.Service migration to remove the deprecated Database export from node.ts
